concept: Exactly **one cup** dominates the center as a heavy matte-black silhouette, industrial and slightly dented, placed in a dirty alley beneath a looming megastructure. The cup overflows with thick bright matte ink (no glow, no bloom), spilling downward in long gravity-driven drips across cracked pavement scattered with cables and trash. As the liquid descends, **a few strands** resolve into simple circuit-glyph traces, symbolic and flat, like printed signal lines rather than realistic circuitry. A faint security scan-line cuts through the scene, hinting at corporate surveillance. A distant silhouette with a visible neck port watches from behind a barrier, reinforcing inequality. The composition must read as a single source of emotion and signal: **1 cup only**, unmistakable and countable, surrounded by heavy blacks, misregistered screenprint texture, urban decay, and globalized industrial density.

Briefcase altar (corporate tension, no vulgarity): an open hard briefcase fills the frame like a rectangular shrine. Inside the case sit **EXACTLY TWO cups**—two large, mirrored industrial silhouettes left and right, same height, clearly separable and countable as 2. Between the two cups rests **ONE** encrypted data-shard/flash-drive key (single object, centered). Across the upper interior edge lies **ONE** contract sheet/card (flat rectangle) with a single bold stamp block (no readable text). Two hands hold the case lid from the inside edges; their fingers reach toward each other and almost touch above the shard, implying charged proximity. Background outside the case is pure negative space / abstract brush blocks only. **COUNT LOCK:** 2 cup shapes total and only inside the case; forbid any other drinkware (bottles, glasses, mugs, teapots), forbid cup-like reflections/puddles, forbid extra shard/keys or extra contract papers; no readable text, no logos.

Make it icon-first: only three forearms/hands enter the frame and **EXACTLY THREE cups** meet in the center, forming a tight triangle clink. Each cup is a big, simple silhouette with thick brush outline, clearly separable and countable as 3. No tables, no bar, no shelves. Confetti is limited to a few paint flecks and torn poster fragments (rectangles/triangles only), never cup-shaped. Background is a minimal rooftop block with one horizon stripe. **COUNT LOCK:** 3 cup shapes total; no extra drinkware, no bottles, no reflections/puddles shaped like cups.

Apathy in an empty whitebox room (no clutter), CP2077 tarot print+brush look. Palette: deep black + 3 inks (muted neon cyan, muted neon magenta, dirty amber) with slight screenprint misregistration; color is matte paint, **no glow/bloom**. A bored figure sits off-center left on a simple block-chair, head turned away. On the floor at bottom-right are **EXACTLY THREE physical cups** arranged in a clean straight row (1-1-1), large matte silhouettes with 2–3 tones max per cup (black mass + one muted ink fill + small accent edge), clearly separable and countable as 3. The **FOURTH cup** exists only as a **single cup-shaped AR icon inside a square projection panel** on the right side; the icon is a flat matte shape (e.g., amber fill with cyan misregistration edge), not luminous. A small **cube projector-drone** in the upper-right emits a rectangular beam; **the drone holds no cup and has no arms**—only the projected square panel contains the cup icon. Background is abstract brush blocks, paper grain, splatter, worn edges. **COUNT LOCK:** exactly 4 cup shapes total = 3 real cups + 1 projected cup icon; forbid any bottles, mugs, glasses, sinks, shelves, bar items, reflections/puddles that look like cups; no readable text, no logos, no watermarks.

Depression after being fired: an ex-corp worker sits slumped beside a cardboard box and a torn ID badge (flat rectangle, no readable text). On the floor: **EXACTLY FIVE cups** and nothing else cup-like. **FOUR cups** are toppled/spilled in the foreground (4 distinct silhouettes), ink pooling as abstract blobs; **ONE cup** stands upright slightly behind and to the side, isolated and cleanly countable. No office kitchen, no bar, no shelves, no extra drinkware. **COUNT LOCK:** 5 cup shapes total (4 fallen + 1 standing); forbid bottles, mugs, cans, glasses, thermos, reflections that read as cups.

Pleasant neuro-sim memories: a person reclines wearing a neural-stim headset. In their hands/near chest: **EXACTLY TWO physical cups** (2 bold silhouettes). Behind their head, within a single glitchy memory frame, appear **EXACTLY FOUR cup-shaped memory emblems** (flat matte icons, no glow) arranged clearly as 2 + 2. The person smiles faintly, calm and soothed. Background is minimal blocks and cable lines only. **COUNT LOCK:** 6 cup shapes total = 2 real cups + 4 memory icons; forbid any other vessels, bottles, mugs, teapot, extra UI icons shaped like cups.

Choice overload with asymmetry (avoid 6): the figure is **off-center left**, half-profile, staring at a battered kiosk **off-center right**. On the kiosk screen show **EXACTLY SEVEN cup silhouettes** arranged as **six cups in a strict 3×2 grid** (clearly separated) **PLUS one larger single cup icon isolated above the grid** (top-right corner of the screen), making **6 + 1 = 7** instantly readable. Each cup icon is a thick matte silhouette with clear spacing and thick outlines; all other UI elements are plain rectangles/lines only (no circular buttons). No physical cups anywhere. Background minimal blocks. **COUNT LOCK:** exactly 7 cup shapes total (icons only); forbid any additional cup-like icons, bottles, mugs, glasses, reflections; no glow/bloom, no readable text.

Leaving Night City for the desert: foreground shows **EXACTLY EIGHT cups** abandoned in a clean arrangement **4 + 4** (two rows), each cup a bold industrial silhouette, clearly countable. A lone traveler walks away toward a dark highway leading into dunes and empty horizon. City skyline is a small block far behind. No other containers, no roadside trash that looks like cups. **COUNT LOCK:** 8 cup shapes total; forbid bottles, canisters, mugs, extra goblets, reflections shaped like cups.

Corporate indulgence (or boss-gang luxury) as a trophy wall: a confident figure sits centered; behind them is a rack with **EXACTLY NINE cups** displayed as trophies in a strict **3×3 grid** (9 distinct silhouettes). The figure may hold a single cigarette/gesture, but **must not hold any cup** (to avoid extra). No bottles, no glasses, no teapot/tea set, no bar clutter. Background reduced to one corporate panel block and a single neon accent shape (flat paint, not glow). **COUNT LOCK:** 9 cup shapes total (rack only); forbid any additional drinkware or cup-shaped decor.

Protected warmth under surveillance: a small chosen-family silhouette group stands below a clean arc of **EXACTLY TEN cups** overhead, arranged **5 left + 5 right** in a symmetrical rainbow-like curve. Each cup is a big, simple silhouette, clearly separable and countable as 10. The ground and background are minimal abstract blocks; add one fence line or scan stripe, but nothing cup-like. No tables, no shelves, no extra lanterns shaped like cups. **COUNT LOCK:** 10 cup shapes total (arc only); forbid any other vessels, bottles, mugs, glasses, reflections.

Curiosity and first intuition: a young netrunner leans toward exactly one cup held or placed at chest height. From the cup rises a koi-shaped AR glyph rendered as a simple flat emblem (matte ink shape, no glow/bloom, no detailed fish). The page’s posture is gentle awe—soft hands, attentive tilt of the head. No additional cups or glassware in the environment; keep it strict: 1 cup total, with rough brush marks, worn edges, and clean negative space around the symbol.

Idealistic mission in motion: a courier rides through night rain carrying exactly one cup protected in a hard case with a clear window/rigid clamp so the single cup silhouette is visibly countable. The protected cup is the focal object—held close or strapped visibly—signaling devotion and message-bearing. Rain is paint streaks and drips, not photoreal. No extra cups on the bike or street. The composition must read 1 cup total, dynamic diagonal movement, gritty screenprint wear and high-contrast silhouette.

Empathy as authority: a calm mediator sits on an industrial seat built from stacked display blocks, holding exactly one cup like a protected heart on the centerline. Her posture is composed and attentive, suggesting emotional intelligence and quiet control. Surroundings are minimal—abstract blocks and brush strokes—so the single cup stays dominant and countable. No extra vessels anywhere. The viewer must read 1 cup total, with heavy blacks, flat shapes, and worn screenprint texture.

A single decisive breakthrough (mono-katana): exactly one sword — a cyberpunk **mono-katana** (slight curve, simple tsuba silhouette, vented spine) — pierces a single data-shard at center, splitting surrounding static into clean signal. The blade is vertical, dominant, and unmistakably the only weapon present. Falling sparks are paint flecks; background shows one abstract megatower block and a tiny drone silhouette. No additional blades or knife shapes anywhere. Composition must read 1 sword total, heavy blacks, worn screenprint texture; matte inks, no glow/bloom.

Stalemate (dual katanas): a blindfolded figure holds **exactly two swords** as **two mono-katanas** crossed in a rigid X over a split road / split panel background. The two blades must be clearly separate and symmetrical—left katana and right katana—no extra knives or edges. Keep hilts minimal (flat wrap blocks), one simple tsuba each (part of the sword, not extra objects). Ensure the count reads **2 swords total**, minimal background detail, heavy blacks, flat shapes, gritty print texture; matte paint, no glow/bloom.

Corporate betrayal (katana trio): a lone corp employee silhouette stands center with a cracked heart-shaped ID-holo panel at the chest (flat emblem, not gory). **EXACTLY THREE swords** pierce that heart-panel: **three mono-katanas** — one vertical center blade, two angled from left/right, all clearly separable and countable. Behind, three colleague silhouettes are hinted only as shoulders/hands offering signatures or turned backs; add torn contract rectangles and a broken badge (no readable text). Background minimal blocks + rain streaks. **COUNT LOCK:** 3 swords total; no extra blades/knives/shards or reflections shaped like swords; matte inks, no glow/bloom.

Forced recovery inside a sterile corporate med-bay: a figure rests on a reinforced slab, visible neck port and chest panel seams. Around them stand **exactly four swords** acting as silent security markers—arranged clearly as **two on the left and two on the right**, each blade separate and countable. No extra weapons or blade-like shapes appear. Overhead lighting forms harsh geometric blocks. Beyond a glass partition, silhouettes of security personnel stand. Palette: deep black + cold teal + faded amber; matte paint, no glow/bloom. The scene must read **4 swords total**, quiet tension beneath clinical stillness, heavy blacks and muted ink tones reinforcing institutional control.

Pyrrhic victory at the top (katanas): a figure stands on a corporate rooftop podium holding **EXACTLY THREE swords** gathered in their arms — three mono-katanas, distinct and countable. Behind them on the ground, **EXACTLY TWO swords** lie abandoned near two retreating silhouettes (distant blocks): two mono-katanas, clearly separate. The winner’s posture is triumphant but hollow; add a cracked laurel-badge panel (flat shape, no text). Background reduced to one skyline block and one spotlight stripe. **COUNT LOCK:** 5 swords total (3 held + 2 on ground); forbid any extra blades, daggers, knife-shapes, or sword-like rails; matte inks, no glow/bloom.

Transition without the boat: a small transit pod / elevator car moves through a dark maintenance corridor toward a brighter exit. Along the corridor walls stand **EXACTLY SIX swords** as rigid waypoint markers: **three on the left wall + three on the right wall**, evenly spaced, each blade distinct and countable. Passengers are reduced to two silhouettes inside the pod. Background is abstract panels and cables only. **COUNT LOCK:** 6 swords total; no extra blades, no knife tools, no sword-like pipes or railings.

Clean theft read (katana bundle): a stealth operative with optical implant carries **EXACTLY FIVE swords** — five mono-katanas bundled in both arms, clearly visible as five distinct hilts/blade edges. Behind them, **EXACTLY TWO swords** remain planted upright as a pair: two mono-katanas, separated from the bundle so the viewer reads **5 + 2 = 7** instantly. Background minimal: one security light cone and one fence line, no clutter. **COUNT LOCK:** 7 swords total; forbid any extra blades/knives/sword-like signage or reflections; matte inks, no glow/bloom.

A netrunner/merc trapped by their own employers: the figure kneels with wrists bound by a single contract-cable (one loop, not complex). Around them stands **EXACTLY EIGHT swords** forming a rigid cage: **four on the left arc + four on the right arc**, evenly spaced, each blade separate and countable. Add one corporate seal block above (no text) implying the “client” owns the cage. Background minimal, heavy negative space. **COUNT LOCK:** 8 swords total; no extra blades, no knife tools, no sword-like bars beyond the eight.

Insomnia under intrusive thoughts (katana silhouettes): a figure sits on a bed with head in hands while **EXACTLY NINE sword silhouettes** loom overhead as a strict **3×3 grid** occupying the upper half of the frame. Each sword silhouette is clearly **katana-shaped** (slight curve, simple guard), with clean separation. Keep background as flat panels only. **COUNT LOCK:** 9 swords total (3×3 only); forbid extra blades, daggers, spikes, or sword-like bedframe parts; matte inks, no glow/bloom.

Terminal collapse beneath systemic weight: a fallen silhouette lies on cracked pavement while **exactly ten swords** are embedded around and into the scene, arranged clearly as **5 on the left and 5 on the right** in a countable band. Each blade is separate and unmistakable. A thin horizon-line suggests distant dawn behind megastructures. No extra blades appear anywhere. Trash and cables surround the body. The card must read **10 swords total**, exhaustion and finality rendered in brutal high-contrast posterized shapes with heavy screenprint wear.

Netrunner trainee: a young analyst stands in a dim server-corridor holding **EXACTLY ONE sword** upright (single blade only). AR overlays appear as flat rectangles, node dots, and wireframe grids around the sword like a scan—**never** forming additional blade shapes. Add one cable bundle and one blinking panel block (no readable text). **COUNT LOCK:** 1 sword total; forbid any extra blades/knives, and avoid sword-like server fins or rails.

No horse (mono-katana charge): a rider on a brutalist hoverbike / street bike cuts through rain, body leaned forward, holding **EXACTLY ONE sword** — a mono-katana thrust ahead. Motion is shown with flat diagonal slashes and spray streaks that never resemble extra blades. Background reduced to one spotlight cone and one barricade block. **COUNT LOCK:** 1 sword total; forbid any additional weapons, knives, or blade-like bike parts; matte inks, no glow/bloom.

Strict corporate exec (katana authority): a cold, perfectly composed corp woman stands before a minimal megacorp interior wall, holding **EXACTLY ONE sword** — a mono-katana — vertical on the centerline like a gavel of truth. Her silhouette is sharp: blazer-armor shoulders, neat hair, subtle temple plate. Background clean blocks and one vertical window stripe; no clutter. **COUNT LOCK:** 1 sword total; forbid any extra blades/knives or sword-like decor; matte inks, no glow/bloom.

Raw ignition in a stratified megacity: **exactly one wand** dominates the center — a heavy industrial power-rod with exposed coils and scorched plating. It erupts with jagged flat sparks rendered as paint splatter, not realistic fire. The rod is clearly the only baton-like object in the scene. A distant megatower skyline and surveillance drone silhouette loom behind. Cables and trash frame the lower edge. A faint prosthetic hand grips the base, visible joint plating. The composition must read **1 wand total**, unmistakable and isolated, heavy blacks and gritty screenprint wear emphasizing ambition igniting within systemic pressure.

COUNT-FIRST layout — two wands as gate posts: a lone operator stands CENTERED between **EXACTLY TWO wands** used as vertical gate posts — **LEFT wand** planted on the far left edge, **RIGHT wand** planted on the far right edge — both at equal height, clearly separated, framing the figure like a doorway. The figure holds with both hands a single flat **globe medallion** at chest level: a round territory-map disc with bold grid meridians and continent-block shapes rendered as flat matte ink (NOT a cup, NOT a pentacle — a FLAT MAP DISC, labeled visually as a globe only by its grid lines). The globe disc is the focal center object between the two wands. Background: one minimal skyline stripe and a single cargo-drone lane; nothing else. Palette: deep black + muted teal ink + dirty amber; matte paint, no glow/bloom, screenprint misregistration. **COUNT LOCK:** 2 wands total (left post + right post only); no extra rods, poles, antennas, flagstaffs; the globe is round but must NOT read as a cup or coin.

Forward momentum after decision: a launch platform scene where **exactly three wands** rise vertically as antenna-like rods in the foreground, clearly countable in a row of **3**. Beyond them, cargo drones streak across the sky as flat motion slashes (never wand-shaped). A lone figure with a visible neck port watches the horizon. No additional rods appear anywhere in the environment. Corporate towers dominate the skyline. The composition must read **3 wands total**, expansion unfolding within a global industrial network, rendered in harsh posterized shapes and worn print texture.

Remote sanctuary far from the city: a quiet Japanese garden / teahouse courtyard at night, city reduced to tiny blocks on the distant horizon. **EXACTLY FOUR wands** form a rigid gate: two vertical on the left, two vertical on the right, supporting one flat lintel panel (panel is a rectangle, not a wand). Ground is raked-sand lines and a few stone silhouettes only (no bamboo, no branches, no lantern poles). A small group silhouette stands beneath the gate. **COUNT LOCK:** 4 wands total; forbid any extra sticks/poles, bamboo stalks, signposts, mic stands, or fence posts.

Active power struggle: a tight street-arena circle where **EXACTLY FIVE wands** clash in the center like a starburst. Five different silhouettes lunge in, each gripping **one** wand; the five rods intersect but remain clearly countable as five distinct sticks (no motion duplicates). Background is minimal: one spotlight cone and rough crowd blocks. **COUNT LOCK:** 5 wands total; forbid any extra batons, poles, flags, spears, railings, or debris that reads as a wand.

No horses, stage victory: a performer/leader stands on a small stage, raising **EXACTLY ONE wand** high like a baton. Behind them, **EXACTLY FIVE wands** stand as vertical banner-rods in a clean row (5 distinct rods, evenly spaced) — total **6**. Audience is block silhouettes behind a barricade line; drone-cameras as small dots (not rod-shaped). **COUNT LOCK:** 6 wands total (1 raised + 5 banners); forbid horses/animals, and forbid any extra poles, mic stands, light stands, or fence posts.

Defiance under pressure: a lone defender stands elevated, gripping **exactly one wand**. From below rise **six incoming wands**, clearly distinct and countable, pointed toward the defender — **1 + 6 = 7 wands total**. Each rod is separate, no extras hidden in background shapes. The defender’s silhouette shows cable-tendon forearms and a reinforced spine plate. Security drones circle above. The scene must read **7 wands total**, courage against overwhelming systemic force rendered in gritty high-contrast print wear.

Count-first speed: **EXACTLY EIGHT wands** streak across the sky in two clean rows **4 + 4**, each a blunt-ended signal baton (no fins, no flames, no missile cues). The eight rods are parallel, separated, and instantly countable; trailing marks are paint flecks only (never rod-shaped). Below is a minimal city block silhouette and one diagonal wind stripe. **COUNT LOCK:** 8 wands total; forbid any additional rods, antennas, poles, cables that read as wands.

Wary endurance in hostile territory: a battered guard with visible prosthetic knee stands gripping **exactly one wand** in the foreground, while **eight wands** form a rigid barricade fence behind them — clearly distinct and countable as **1 + 8 = 9 wands total**. Each rod is separate and unmistakable. Trash, cables, and warning stripes line the ground. A distant corporate checkpoint looms cold beyond the barricade. No additional rods appear anywhere. The composition must read **9 wands total**, resilience against systemic pressure rendered in heavy blacks and gritty print texture.

Crushing overload in a stratified city: a hunched figure hauls **exactly ten wands** bundled tightly together, the ends clearly visible and countable as **10 distinct rod tips**. No extra rods appear anywhere in the environment. The bundle obscures the figure’s face, revealing only a reinforced neck plate. Corporate towers loom overhead. Trash and shipping containers frame the lower edge. The scene must read **10 wands total**, weight and systemic burden expressed through heavy black mass and rough screenprint grime.

Curious experimentation in a back-alley workshop: a rookie engineer with visible optical implant examines **exactly one wand-like tool**, the only rod in the frame. The tool emits jagged flat sparks rendered as paint flecks, never forming additional rods. The workshop is cluttered with cables, broken panels, and shipping crates. No other batons appear anywhere. The composition must read **1 wand total**, creative potential emerging within urban decay and corporate neglect, rendered in heavy blacks and gritty screenprint texture.

Knight energy without a bike: an armored street duelist in a light exosuit launches in a long leap between rooftops, cape/coat trailing, holding **EXACTLY ONE wand** like a heated power-lance. The single rod is dominant and countable; motion is shown with flat slashes and dust streaks that never look like extra rods. Background minimal: one rooftop edge and one spotlight block. **COUNT LOCK:** 1 wand total; forbid motorcycles, horses/animals, and forbid any extra batons/poles.

Charismatic club icon: a queen-performer commands a small stage, executing an athletic aerial/pole-acrobat pose around **EXACTLY ONE vertical wand** (the wand is the performance pole — a single rod, central and countable). Audience silhouettes and drone-cameras are reduced to dots/blocks only. No other poles, mic stands, light stands, or railings. **COUNT LOCK:** 1 wand total; forbid any additional rods/poles/batons.

Elite conspiracy (Alpha Centauri vibe, subtle): exactly one pentacle dominates the center — a single round coin/chip with a bold pentagram-like glyph (matte ink, **no glow/bloom**). CP2077 tarot print+brush palette: deep black + muted neon cyan + muted neon magenta + dirty amber, slight screenprint misregistration. The coin is presented not from the sky but from a **dark triangular aperture** cut into a monolithic black ‘screen’ (suggesting a hidden council). Inside the aperture, show **one** minimal eye-like oval glyph as a flat paint mark (symbolic, not detailed), and **one** simplified hand silhouette releasing the coin. Below: a tiny street market silhouette behind fencing, one prosthetic hand reaching up. No other coins, badges, pentacle icons, triangles-within-triangles, or repeating symbols—keep it singular. Composition must read **1 pentacle total**, heavy blacks, worn screenprint texture, paper grain, splatter, smudges, clean negative space.

Precarious balance in a volatile economy: a figure juggles exactly two pentacles — two distinct round tokens/chips, each with the same bold pentagram-like glyph. They move inside a single flat infinity-loop brush stroke. No additional coin icons, UI badges, or extra tokens anywhere. Palette: deep black + muted amber + industrial teal; matte paint, no glow/bloom. Composition must read 2 pentacles total, countable and separated, heavy blacks and muted ink tones.

Corporate infrastructure, not a workshop: inside a high-ceiling megacorp atrium, three specialists confer over a floating blueprint slab (flat rectangle, no readable text). Above them on a wall panel are **EXACTLY THREE pentacles** as separate round emblems with a bold pentagram-like glyph, clearly countable as 3. The three people are distinct roles by silhouette: one architect with tablet, one engineer with tool-glove, one suited manager with badge. Background is clean brutalist blocks and a single glass line; no benches of tools, no repair clutter. **COUNT LOCK:** 3 pentacles total; forbid any additional coin icons, circular badges, or extra emblems.

Possession and fear: a rigid figure clutches a vault stamped with exactly four pentacles. One large pentacle seal sits on the vault door; three smaller pentacle seals lock around the figure (one under the feet, two near the shoulders). Each is a distinct round emblem with a bold pentagram-like glyph. No other badges/coins appear. Palette: deep black + muted amber + rust orange; matte paint, no glow/bloom. Composition must read 4 pentacles total, heavy blacks and muted ink tones.

Exclusion from money itself: cold rain outside a bank branch window. Inside the glass, **EXACTLY FIVE pentacles** appear as flat matte paint emblems (bold pentagram-like glyph, no glow/bloom), arranged clearly and countably as 5. Outside, two silhouettes with patched cyberware stand under the overhang, turned away from warmth. Keep all other signage as rectangles only (no circular logos). **COUNT LOCK:** 5 pentacles total; forbid any other coin/badge shapes, no extra round lights that could be read as pentacles.

Controlled generosity with clear cast: a suited executive stands elevated on steps. They release **EXACTLY SIX pentacles** — six separate round chips with a bold pentagram-like glyph — arranged clearly as **3 falling toward the left + 3 falling toward the right** (3+3). On the left, a worker silhouette with a tool-belt and prosthetic hand reaches up; on the right, a netrunner silhouette with hood and optical implant reaches up. A security guard silhouette stands behind the executive as a block shape. Background is minimal, one barrier line. **COUNT LOCK:** 6 pentacles total; forbid any extra coin icons, badges, or circular lights.

Delayed return in a synth-production lab: behind glass, a rack of sealed reactor pods produces synthetic product (clean canisters and tubes, abstract, not plants). Attached to the pods are **EXACTLY SEVEN pentacles** as round inspection tags with a bold pentagram-like glyph, clearly countable as 7. A worker silhouette with neck plating pauses, evaluating the batch with a clipboard rectangle (no text). Keep all other labels as plain rectangles, no extra round icons. **COUNT LOCK:** 7 pentacles total; forbid any additional pentacle emblems, coins, or circular signage.

Cyberpunk repetition, not a workshop: a lone operator in a micro-factory control bay monitors an automated arm through glass. On the far wall is a display rack showing **EXACTLY EIGHT pentacles** as finished certification plates, arranged clearly **4 + 4**. Each pentacle is a distinct round emblem with a bold pentagram-like glyph. The operator silhouette holds a small console slab (rectangle). No hand tools, no benches, no repair scene. **COUNT LOCK:** 8 pentacles total; forbid any extra coin/badge shapes, circular warning lights, or additional emblems.

Moon refuge: a lone figure stands inside a small pressurized dome on the lunar surface, Earth faint and distant in the sky. Along the dome’s inner frame, **EXACTLY NINE pentacles** are mounted as a strict **3×3 grid** of round emblems with a bold pentagram-like glyph, clearly countable as 9. Keep all other lights as rectangles only. Outside the dome: minimal moon dust horizon and one antenna block (not circular). **COUNT LOCK:** 9 pentacles total; forbid any additional coin/badge shapes or extra round lights.

Dynastic legacy gathering — three generations in the ritual hall: a grand underground salon of the family dynasty. At the far end stands an ANCESTOR WALL: a wide stone-block panel with **EXACTLY TEN pentacles** mounted as flat round seals in two clean rows of **5 + 5**, each with a bold pentagram-like glyph — the accumulated crests of the lineage, one per generation of achievement. Before the wall stand **THREE generational silhouettes**: one elder (wide, stooped, ceremonial coat with crown-ridge implant marking the patriarch), one adult (upright, structured silhouette), and at the front one small child figure — the inheritor. All three share the same **family-mark implant** on their faces, but scaled in size to their rank: elder has the largest crown-ridge, child has the smallest stub, signaling bloodline continuity. The adult rests one hand on the child's shoulder; the elder faces the wall, presenting the lineage. The atmosphere is warm and solemn — NOT threatening. No ritual supplicants, no kneeling strangers. Background: ancestor wall with the ten pentacle-crests centered, heavy curtain blocks flanking, warm floor lines. Palette: deep black + warm amber + aged ivory; matte paint, no glow/bloom, screenprint grain. **COUNT LOCK:** 10 pentacle seals total (wall only); forbid any additional coins, circular logos, or extra round lights anywhere else in the frame.

Beginner fixer: a young street fixer studies **EXACTLY ONE pentacle** — a single round chip with a bold pentagram-like glyph — held in a gloved hand at chest height, countable and isolated. Around it float AR deal-notes as flat rectangles and checkmarks (no circular icons). Background: a backroom booth silhouette and one city window stripe. No extra tokens, coins, or badges anywhere. **COUNT LOCK:** 1 pentacle total.

Steady duty in logistics: a delivery worker crosses container yards carrying a crate stamped with exactly one pentacle — one round emblem with a bold pentagram-like glyph on the main crate face. No other circular stamps or badges on the scene. Palette: deep black + industrial teal + worn amber; matte paint, no glow/bloom. Composition must read 1 pentacle total, heavy blacks and muted ink tones.

Matron of the dynasty — throne room as sanctuary: the QUEEN sits elevated on a wide ceremonial throne-block at center, her seated silhouette dominant and calm. Her face is covered by a **full-face nurturing-authority implant**: a flat matte mask-panel with a sharp **crown-ridge** along the top edge — the society's matriarch mark, built into the implant itself. Unlike the cold authority of the initiators, her posture is OPEN: both arms rest forward on the armrests, palms upward in a gesture of giving. In her right open palm rests **EXACTLY ONE pentacle** — a large round seal with bold pentagram-like glyph — presented freely outward, offered as a gift, not clutched. At the base of her throne, **three smaller attendant silhouettes** sit or crouch in a semi-circle, leaning toward her rather than bowing away — they are being provided for, sheltered. Background: two heavy curtain-column blocks flanking the throne, one horizontal floor-line, heavy warm shadows filling the corners. Palette: deep black + warm amber + aged ivory; matte paint, no glow/bloom, screenprint grain. The amber palette — not cold violet — conveys earthly warmth, stability, abundance. **COUNT LOCK:** 1 pentacle total; crown-ridge is angular crest only; forbid any other coins, circular logos, extra round lights.
